The Little Mermaid (2023)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $298,172,056Details
International Box Office $271,454,233
Worldwide Box Office $569,626,289
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,083,350
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,023,189
Total $5,106,539
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Ariel is a beautiful and spirited young mermaid with a thirst for adventure. The youngest of King Triton’s daughters and the most defiant, Ariel longs to find out more about the world beyond the sea and, while visiting the surface, falls for the dashing Prince Eric. While mermaids are forbidden to interact with humans, Ariel must follow her heart. She makes a deal with the evil sea witch, Ursula, which gives her a chance to experience life on land but ultimately places her life—and her father’s crown—in jeopardy.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$95,578,040 (32.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.12 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:52.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$250,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,320 opening theaters/4,320 max. theaters, 6.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $325,972,371

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 26th, 2023 (Wide) by Walt Disney
May 26th, 2023 (IMAX) by Walt Disney
International Releases: May 24th, 2023 (Wide) (Belgium)
May 24th, 2023 (Wide), released as La Petite Sirène (France)
May 24th, 2023 (Wide), released as La Sirenetta (Italy)
May 24th, 2023 (Wide) (Indonesia)
May 24th, 2023 (Wide) (Netherlands)

Running Time: 135 minutes
Franchise: The Little Mermaid
Cast, crew, or production detail: Live Action Remake, Remake

Synopsis

Ariel is a beautiful and spirited young mermaid with a thirst for adventure. The youngest of King Triton’s daughters and the most defiant, Ariel longs to find out more about the world beyond the sea and, while visiting the surface, falls for the dashing Prince Eric. While mermaids are forbidden to interact with humans, Ariel must follow her heart. She makes a deal with the evil sea witch, Ursula, which gives her a chance to experience life on land but ultimately places her life—and her father’s crown—in jeopardy.
